title: Readiness and Mastery Dashboard
description: Given an eight-node sample dependency graph and a slider-controlled mastery state for each node, the learner can predict and verify which nodes become part of the readiness frontier as mastery thresholds are crossed, and observe how a learning path constraint reorders the final personalized sequence.
creator: Learning Graphs
author: Dan McCreary
subject: Learning Graphs

educational.topic: Learner Modeling, Readiness Estimation, Personalized Sequencing
learningObjectives: Predict which knowledge components enter the readiness frontier as mastery values cross the threshold
Explain why the readiness frontier is non-empty at zero mastery
Explain why raising the mastery threshold can remove a component from 'mastered'
Explain why a learning path constraint can reorder some pairs but never violate a prerequisite

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Live slider-driven simulation with predict-then-verify and a constraint toggle
keyQuestions: Why is the readiness frontier non-empty when every mastery value is 0%?
Master Graph alone: which components become ready? Now master Directed Edge too. Why did the answer change?
If every component sits at 75%, what happens when the threshold moves from 70% to 80%? What does that say about reporting 'mastery' without a threshold?
Could any constraint ever place Prerequisite before DAG? Why not?
commonMisconceptions: That the readiness frontier is empty until the learner masters something — root concepts are vacuously ready
That mastery is a property of the learner rather than a claim relative to a threshold
That a learning path constraint can override a prerequisite — it can only choose among orderings the graph already permits
That the recommended sequence is fixed — it is recomputed from the learner model on every change
assessmentOpportunities: Have learners predict the frontier before dragging each slider, then verify
Set all components to 75% and move the threshold across 75% to show mastery being revoked
Ask learners to identify which pairs the constraint could legitimately reorder and which it could not
